2603.23043.title: Assessing the Robustness of Climate Foundation Models under No-Analog Distribution Shifts
2603.23043.paper_summary: The accelerating pace of climate change introduces profound non-stationarities that challenge the ability of Machine Learning based climate emulators to generalize beyond their training distributions. While these emulators offer computationally efficient alternatives to traditional Earth System Models, their reliability remains a potential bottleneck under "no-analog" future climate states, which we define here as regimes where external forcing drives the system into conditions outside the empirical range of the historical training data. A fundamental challenge in evaluating this reliability is data contamination; because many models are trained on simulations that already encompass future scenarios, true out-of-distribution (OOD) performance is often masked. To address this, we benchmark the OOD robustness of three state-of-the-art architectures: U-Net, ConvLSTM, and the ClimaX foundation model specifically restricted to a historical-only training regime (1850-2014). We evaluate these models using two complementary strategies: (i) temporal extrapolation to the recent climate (2015-2023) and (ii) cross-scenario forcing shifts across divergent emission pathways. Our analysis within this experimental setup reveals an accuracy vs. stability trade-off: while the ClimaX foundation model achieves the lowest absolute error, it exhibits higher relative performance changes under distribution shifts, with precipitation errors increasing by up to 8.44% under extreme forcing scenarios. These findings suggest that when restricted to historical training dynamics, even high-capacity foundation models are sensitive to external forcing trajectories. Our results underscore the necessity of scenario-aware training and rigorous OOD evaluation protocols to ensure the robustness of climate emulators under a changing climate.
2603.23043.paper_summary_zh: 氣候變化加速的步伐引入了深刻的非平穩性，這挑戰了基於機器學習的氣候模擬器在其訓練分佈之外進行概括的能力。雖然這些模擬器提供了對傳統地球系統模型的計算效率替代方案，但在「無類似」的未來氣候狀態下，其可靠性仍然是一個潛在的瓶頸，我們在此將其定義為外部驅動力將系統推入超出歷史訓練數據經驗範圍的條件。評估這種可靠性的一個根本挑戰是數據污染；因為許多模型是在已經涵蓋未來情景的模擬上訓練的，因此真正的分佈外（OOD）性能通常被掩蓋。為了解決這個問題，我們基準測試了三種最先進架構的OOD穩健性：U-Net、ConvLSTM，以及特別限制於歷史訓練範疇（1850-2014）的ClimaX基礎模型。我們使用兩種互補策略來評估這些模型：（i）對最近氣候（2015-2023）的時間外推，和（ii）在不同排放路徑之間的跨情景驅動力轉變。我們在這個實驗設置中的分析揭示了準確性與穩定性之間的權衡：雖然ClimaX基礎模型達到了最低的絕對誤差，但在分佈轉變下，它的相對性能變化較大，極端驅動場景下降水誤差增加了多達8.44%。這些發現表明，當限制於歷史訓練動態時，即使是高容量的基礎模型對外部驅動路徑也很敏感。我們的結果強調了情景感知訓練和嚴格的OOD評估協議的必要性，以確保氣候模擬器在變化中的氣候下的穩健性。
2603.23043.author: Maria Conchita Agana Navarro et.al.
2603.23043.authors: Maria Conchita Agana Navarro, Geng Li, Theo Wolf, Maria Perez-Ortiz
